reviewer: agent build + matrix pass (revision 1) — headless Chrome, real inference + mobile/desktop screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Headless Chrome (own driver, WASM no-GPU): first-visit Download (~67 MB) → real output "clickbait / manipulative" 99.8% in 50 ms on WASM. probs manipulative 99.8% / legitimate 0.2% sum to 100%. console + network clean. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/clickbait-detection/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/clickbait-detection/mobile.png
rubric.notes: Model CoolpantsMcBadass/headline-classifier loaded via transformers.js pipeline in a Web Worker; verified against real headlines (curiosity-gap → manipulative, straight news → legitimate).

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): io-shape-semantic=manual. AGENT VERDICT: labelled 2-class softmax (manipulative + legitimate) that sums to 1.00 and matches the headline ("You won't believe…" → manipulative 99.8%; "Federal Reserve raises rates" → legitimate 99.7% in Node cross-check).
rubric.notes: Node runnability pre-check (transformers.js@3.7.5) confirmed real labels on 5 headlines before build.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (CoolpantsMcBadass/headline-classifier linked), declares-task=pass (text-classification), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (worker.js).

conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader). AGENT VERDICT: cached second visit auto-inits to ready with NO Download button and a "Clear cached model" control present (verified in the same headless browser session after first download).

conformance: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass (.err status + Retry via shared loader). honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback=manual — WASM path runs with no GPU, honest download progress observed.

conformance: controls-operable=manual. AGENT VERDICT: sample chips set the textarea + re-score (debounced), textarea drives as-you-type scoring, practical threshold slider re-triages the queue, wild gauntlet + batch buttons run — all real, labelled, keyboard-operable.
conformance: no-fake-output=manual. AGENT VERDICT: every score comes from the on-device worker run; the cue chips are labelled a UI teaching aid (not the model's features); no canned results.
conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Loader status is role=status aria-live=polite; textarea/range/buttons labelled.

conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. AGENT VERDICT: desktop 1280×800 + mobile 360×740 screenshots read — no horizontal overflow, legible text, model-id link wraps, prob bars + meter render, tap targets adequate. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/clickbait-detection/
conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs off the main thread (Web Worker) and as-you-type scoring is debounced (350 ms), so typing stays responsive; batch scoring is one padded forward pass.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs off the main thread via a Web Worker; the control UI stays responsive and as-you-type scoring is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread = pass; worker.js runs pipeline()

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Identify slow JS impacting INP; keep interaction handlers light.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the input handler only schedules a debounced worker call; no synchronous model work on the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0; auto-fit minmax() grids; logical properties — no fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls-grid/pair-grid use repeat(auto-fit,minmax()); prob-bar uses min-inline-size:0; ticket .msg uses flex:1 1 200px; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop+mobile no horizontal overflow; screenshots reports/responsive/screens/clickbait-detection/

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference state in a role=status aria-live region; every control labelled + keyboard operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader status is role=status aria-live=polite; textarea/range/buttons labelled; sample chips are real <button type=button>.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate visible labels with textarea/range controls; keep sample chips focusable buttons.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every input has an associated <label>; threshold slider shows its live value; chips are <button type=button>.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless run drove chips + slider + batch buttons; conformance controls checks pass

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system, invariant 6).

openQuestions: Clickbait has no crisp surface signal; punchy-but-real headlines can trip the flag. Is the "signal not verdict" framing (and the wild gauntlet's surprise counter) clear enough that users don't treat the score as ground truth?
The model is English-only (DistilBERT uncased); non-English headlines are out of scope — worth surfacing that limit more prominently?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a browser-extension-style overlay demo that badges clickbait headlines in a live-rendered mock feed, as a wild/practical composition.

summary: Revision-1 agent build + matrix pass for clickbait-detection (text-classification, wasm): real on-device inference verified in headless Chrome ("clickbait / manipulative" 99.8%, 50 ms WASM, probs sum to 1.00), cached auto-init confirmed (no Load button, Clear-cache present), mobile+desktop screenshots read (no overflow, legible), console+network clean. All rubric dimensions evidenced; support flipped to ok.
